info.name: Fuel Route Optimizer API
info.description:
POST /api/route -- given a start and finish location within the USA, returns the driving route, the cost-optimal set of fuel stops along it (500-mile max range, 10 mpg), and the total fuel cost.

All requests target `{{base_url}}/api/route` (override `base_url` per-environment; defaults to `http://localhost`, the Docker Compose deployment served by nginx on port 80. For a local `manage.py runserver` instead, set `base_url` to `http://localhost:8000`).

Request body: `{"start": <location>, "finish": <location>}`. Each of `start`/`finish` independently accepts:
- a `"lat,lng"` string (coordinate order is **lat,lng**, the human/Google-Maps convention -- NOT GeoJSON's lng,lat)
- a `[lat, lng]` pair
- a free-text US address string

The server sniffs the shape -- no type tag is required. Coordinate input costs 1 Mapbox Directions call; every address in the request adds one forward-geocoding call (2 addresses = 3 calls total), so the coordinate path is the 1-call ideal and the address path is the 3-call ceiling.

Error envelope on any 4xx/5xx: `{"error": {"code": "<machine_code>", "message": "<human>", "detail": {...}}}`.

## Run order for the cache-hit demo
Run "1. Coordinate happy path" first, then immediately run "4. Cache hit (repeat of #1)". The second call returns the identical payload with zero additional Mapbox calls -- watch the response time drop and, if a server-side call counter/log is visible during the Loom walkthrough, confirm no new Directions call fired.

## 502 upstream error (not included as a live request)
A Mapbox transport failure (network error, non-200, or a missing/invalid `MAPBOX_TOKEN`) maps to `502` with `error.code == "upstream_error"`. This is impractical to trigger on demand against a live Mapbox account, so it is not included as a saved request here -- it is proven end-to-end by an automated endpoint test with a stubbed Mapbox transport failure (`routing/tests/test_views.py::RouteViewDomainErrorTests::test_upstream_transport_failure_returns_502_with_no_token_leak`). It is optionally demoable live by temporarily unsetting `MAPBOX_TOKEN` and re-running any request in this collection.

item.name: 1. Coordinate happy path (1 call, forces a fuel stop)

request.description: Denver, CO -> Kansas City, MO -- both endpoints given as `lat,lng` coordinates, so this makes exactly 1 Mapbox Directions call and zero geocoding calls (the 1-call ideal). Real driving distance is ~560 mi via I-70, which exceeds the 500-mi tank range, so the cheapest feasible plan requires at least one fuel stop (non-empty `fuel_stops`) -- verified against this project's own station dataset (max corridor gap along this route is ~180 mi, well under the 500-mi range, so the trip is feasible with real, on-route Colorado/Kansas travel centers). Also asserts the response carries `map_url` and route geometry.

exec: pm.test("fuel_stops is a non-empty array", function () {
pm.test("response carries route_geometry, total_cost, total_gallons, total_route_mi, map_url", function () {

request.description: Empower Field at Mile High, Denver -> Arrowhead Stadium, Kansas City -- both endpoints given as free-text US addresses. Costs 2 forward-geocoding calls (one per address) + 1 Directions call = 3 total, the external-call ceiling.

exec: pm.test("response carries fuel_stops and total_cost", function () {

request.description: Empower Field at Mile High, Denver (address) -> Kansas City, MO (coordinate). One field is an address, the other a coordinate -- costs 1 forward-geocoding call + 1 Directions call = 2 total (the middle tier).

Byte-for-byte the same body as "1. Coordinate happy path". Run #1 first, then this -- the normalized cache key matches, so this request is served from cache with zero additional Mapbox calls and an identical response body. Watch the response time: the first call includes a live Directions round-trip, this one does not.

exec: pm.test("response body is identical to request #1 (cache hit)", function () {
console.log("Run '1. Coordinate happy path' first to compare cache-hit equality.");

request.description: `finish` is omitted entirely. The request serializer rejects this before any Mapbox call is made -- 400 with error.code `invalid_input`.

exec: pm.test("error envelope has code invalid_input", function () {

request.description: `start` is Regina, Saskatchewan (Canada) -- outside the continental-US bounding box, even though it is close enough that a naive country check might miss it. Rejected at the serializer before any Mapbox call -- 400 with error.code `invalid_input` or `out_of_bounds`.

exec: pm.test("error envelope code is invalid_input or out_of_bounds", function () {

request.description: Catalina Island, CA (Avalon) -> Los Angeles, CA. Both points are well-formed US coordinates, but Catalina is a ferry-only island whose road network is disconnected from the mainland -- Mapbox Directions' driving profile finds no route between them (returns code `NoRoute`). The API maps this to 422 with error.code `route_not_found`. Guaranteed-reproducible proof of this mapping (independent of live Mapbox road data) is `routing/tests/test_views.py::RouteViewDomainErrorTests::test_no_route_returns_422_route_not_found`.

exec: pm.test("error envelope has code route_not_found", function () {

request.description: San Francisco, CA -> Seattle, WA via I-5 (~680 mi). Verified against this project's own station dataset: nearly every routable California station sits in the far southeast corner near the Mexican border, so the I-5 corridor through California and southern Oregon carries no candidate stations for roughly the first 510 miles of this trip -- a gap greater than the 500-mile tank range with no reachable station in between. Returns 422 with error.code `infeasible_route` and a structured gap detail (`from_station`, `to_station`, `gap_mi`, `max_range_mi`). Guaranteed-reproducible proof of this mapping is `routing/tests/test_views.py::RouteViewDomainErrorTests::test_gap_exceeding_range_returns_422_infeasible_route`.

exec: pm.test("error envelope has code infeasible_route", function () {
pm.test("error detail carries gap fields", function () {

item.name: 9. Multi-stop happy path (1 call, several fuel stops)

request.description: Dallas, TX -> Los Angeles, CA -- both endpoints given as `lat,lng` coordinates, so this makes exactly 1 Mapbox Directions call and zero geocoding calls (the 1-call ideal). The ~1,437-mi trip far exceeds the 500-mi tank range, so the cheapest feasible plan spans several fuel stops (multiple `fuel_stops`) -- verified against this project's own station dataset (~5 stops). Shares the finish coordinate with scenario 7 (Catalina -> LA) for consistency, and uses the same fixed coords as the SPA's Dallas -> Los Angeles demo preset.

exec: pm.test("fuel_stops has more than one stop", function () {
pm.test("response carries total_cost and map_url", function () {

request.description: Dallas, TX -> Los Angeles, CA with a nested `vehicle` object describing a loaded semi (6 mpg, a 300-mi tank, and a half-full starting tank). The response echoes the resolved profile -- including the derived `starting_fuel_mi` -- and the smaller effective range forces more, closer-together fuel stops than the default 500-mi/10-mpg profile on the same trip (compare against "9. Multi-stop happy path"). Also asserts every stop carries a structured `rationale` object and that `legs`/`savings` are populated.

exec: pm.test("echoes the resolved vehicle profile including starting_fuel_mi", function () {
pm.test("has legs, savings, and per-stop rationale", function () {

request.description: San Francisco, CA -> Los Angeles, CA -- a corridor with legitimate alternate driving routes (I-5 direct vs. US-101 coastal vs. CA-99), a reasonable candidate for Mapbox to actually return more than one alternative. Asserts `alternatives_considered` matches the length of the `alternatives` array and that exactly one entry is marked `chosen`. Mapbox returns 0-2 alternatives depending on live route geometry; whichever demo trip is confirmed (during phase verification) to reliably yield more than one alternative should replace these coordinates before the Loom walkthrough.

exec: pm.test("reports alternatives_considered and exactly one chosen alternative", function () {
